concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains several individual images of rock and mineral specimens on page 54 (numbered 5 through 10). In the HTML output, images 5 and 6 are missing entirely as elements and are instead represented only by text placeholders ('[Image: ...]'). Additionally, the extraction of images on this page appears corrupted, as some images are missing or incorrectly grouped into large multi-panel fragments.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 54 shows six distinct images of rock and mineral specimens numbered 5 through 10.
concerns.output_quote:
[Image: A collection of loose, small grey and brown rocks and gravel.]

[Image: A solid white rock or mineral specimen displayed next to a dark coin for size comparison.]

concerns.analysis: The multiple-choice questions starting on page 58 contain a structural list defect. In several questions, the sub-items (answer choices) are incorrectly flattened to the same level as the parent item (the question) rather than being nested as children. For example, in Question 3, 'a. iron nail' is a child, but 'b. penny', 'c. fingernail', and 'd. all of the above' are extracted as siblings of the question itself. In Question 14, all answer choices are extracted as siblings of the question. This
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
3. Which of the following could be used to test the hardness of a mineral?
a. iron nail
b. penny
c. fingernail
d. all of the above
concerns.output_quote: {"text": "Which of the following could be used to test the hardness of a mineral?", "children": [{"text": "a. iron nail"}]}, {"text": "b. penny"}, {"text": "c. fingernail"}, {"text": "d. all of the above"}

concerns.description: Missing table(s): source PDF has tables not found in output on p5(source:1,output:0), p29(source:1,output:0), p36(source:1,output:0), p38(source:1,output:0)
concerns.analysis: source_tables=13, output_tables=12. Pages with missing tables: p5(source:1,output:0), p29(source:1,output:0), p36(source:1,output:0), p38(source:1,output:0)

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 3 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=3. Examples: p28: "MOHS SCALE OF HARDNESS"; p35: "Mohs Hardness Scale With Examples of Common Tests"; p37: "Complete Mohs Hardness Scale With Examples of Common Tests". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Missing images: 4 of 12 source image(s) not present in output
concerns.analysis: image_count_source=12, html_img_count=8, image_count_ratio=1.0. Some source images are missing from the output.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 4 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=4. Examples: p54: "A two-panel display. The top partially visible pan"; p54: "A document page featuring two framed sections with"; p54: "Two framed panels, with the top showing three irre". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 16 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=16, examples=['http://sargentwelch.com/sargent-welch-introductory-rock-coll', 'http://www.project2061.org/publications/bsl/online/bointro.h', 'http://sargentwelch.com/sargent-welch-introductory-rock-coll']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 15 ordered list(s) have alphabetical markers (A, B, C) rendered as numeric — e.g. "a. hardness"; "a. granite"; "Which of the following could be used to test the hardness of"
concerns.analysis: alpha_numeric_confusion=15. Ordered list items start with sequential letter prefixes (A., B., C.) but are rendered as numeric lists, losing the alphabetical semantics.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Unit Title: Rocks and Minerals"; 5 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1, no_th_tables=5. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 1 form(s) with 2 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=1, total_fields=2. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 8 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (8 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p53: "... small black crystals, set against a solid reddish"; p53: "...bed of dry leaves. The mostly light grey, speckled"; p53: "... light and dark minerals, characteristic of gneiss"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=8 (truncated=8, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Table row count mismatch: p28(source:8rows,output:12rows), p35(source:14rows,output:23rows), p37(source:5rows,output:12rows)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have >20% row count difference (delta >=3). Pages: p28(source:8rows,output:12rows), p35(source:14rows,output:23rows), p37(source:5rows,output:12rows)

concerns.description: Table column count mismatch: p2(source:4cols,output:3cols), p35(source:4cols,output:3cols), p37(source:4cols,output:3cols)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have different column counts. Pages: p2(source:4cols,output:3cols), p35(source:4cols,output:3cols), p37(source:4cols,output:3cols)
Text consistently appears as first or last element on 2/61 pages — likely a running header/footer
header_footer_count=58. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.
